# CARE v0.2.0-alpha

CARE turns piles of police crash report PDFs into **structured, redacted** research artifacts — diagrams and narratives you can study without ever moving the original PII out of your workstation. This alpha is the first public preview of the desktop GUI plus the new plugin-accuracy panel.

> **Pre-1.0.** Core invariants (offline-first, fail-closed, plugin-based, no original PDFs in public exports) are stable. The API and CLI surface may still change.

---

## Which installer should I download?

Pick the row that matches your network situation and whether you plan to use the optional ML plugins. **For most users on a connected workstation, `CARE-0.2.0-core-online-Setup.exe` is the right choice.**

| File | When to use |
|---|---|
| `CARE-0.2.0-core-online-Setup.exe` | **Default.** Slim install (~120 MB). Connected workstation. WebView2 runtime is fetched automatically if missing. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-core-airgap-Setup.exe` | Same as above but bundles the WebView2 runtime offline (~150 MB extra). Pick this if your machine cannot reach Microsoft's update servers. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-ml-online-Setup.exe` | Adds `torch` + `transformers` (~2 GB) so optional Hugging Face plugins can run. **Model weights are not bundled** — install them separately, see below. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-ml-airgap-Setup.exe` | Same ML libraries with the offline WebView2 runtime. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-core.msi` | Enterprise SCCM / Intune deployment, slim. WebView2 is a documented prerequisite admins pre-stage. |
| `CARE-0.2.0-ml.msi` | SCCM / Intune deployment with the ML libraries. |

Per-user install (no admin) is the default for the `.exe` SKUs. The installer dialog lets you elevate to per-machine if you want every account on the workstation to see the app. The `.msi` SKUs are per-machine only.

---

## What's new since v0.1.0-alpha

**GUI**
- **Task-oriented navigation** — Process / Review / Export are the primary tabs; Plugins, Templates, Settings, and Diagnostics live under **Advanced**.
- **Drawer-driven New Run** with explicit jurisdiction vs. template-id help and live job polling.
- **Three-tab review queue** (Needs attention / Approved / Rejected / All) with QA flags surfaced as plain English in a top-concern column.
- **Two-pane report detail** — redacted crash diagram on the left, officer's narrative with inline PII highlights on the right, and a synced inventory sidebar.
- **Plugin chain reorder** with section-aware semantics: OCR uses Primary / Fallback labels (strict fallback ordering); PII and Document-AI use #N (positional only).
- **Always-visible offline status indicator** that jumps to the offline section of Settings on click.

**Plugin transparency** *(new in this build)*
- Per-provider **accuracy column** with a tier badge so claims can't outrank evidence:
  - **Tier A** — project-run benchmark on a held-out crash-narrative corpus.
  - **Tier B** — published in-domain numbers from the model's source paper.
  - **Tier C** — vendor / unverified numbers.
  - The UI ranks providers within the same tier only.
- The shipped regex PII provider declares Tier A numbers (headline F1 ≈ 0.91; structured PII like VIN / phone / plate at 1.0; PERSON_NAME at 0.69 — exactly where regex would be expected to lag a learned model).
- Hover the Accuracy cell for the per-entity breakdown and the benchmark version.

**Backend / runtime**
- FastAPI lifespan **actually activates the offline guard** at app startup.
- Static frontend assets served with `Cache-Control: no-store`, fixing stale-cache problems when GUI assets change.
- Windows: `pythonnet` is now declared platform-conditionally and loaded before `webview.start()` so the WinForms backend imports `clr` cleanly.

**Security**
- CodeQL hardening — workflow permissions tightened, prototype-pollution path fixed, loopback assertion strengthened.

---

## Installing optional ML model weights

The `ml` SKU bundles `torch` + `transformers`, but it does **not** include any model weights — those are licence-gated and operator-controlled by design. After installing the `ml` SKU:

1. Read [`docs/license-and-model-governance.md`](https://github.com/AhmedAredah/care/blob/main/docs/license-and-model-governance.md) for each model you intend to use.
2. Download the weights manually from the upstream source (Hugging Face, Paddle, etc.) on a machine that's allowed to.
3. Drop them under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\CARE\models\<category>\<provider-name>\` (e.g. `%LOCALAPPDATA%\CARE\models\pii\piiranha\`).
4. Enable the provider in the **Plugins** page; CARE refuses to load until model files are present and config-named.

Piiranha and Kosmos-2.5 are flagged **license-review-required**; the GUI shows a warning chip until you've confirmed your organisation has authority to deploy them.

---

## SmartScreen / Windows Defender

These alpha binaries are **unsigned**. Code signing is provided free of charge to OSS by the [SignPath Foundation](https://signpath.io/foundation); CARE's enrolment is in progress. Until it completes, Windows SmartScreen will warn on first launch.

Two equivalent ways to clear the warning:

- **In Explorer:** right-click the downloaded `.exe` → **Properties** → tick **Unblock** → **OK**, then run normally.
- **In the SmartScreen dialog:** click **More info** → **Run anyway**.

Both options should only be used **after** verifying the SHA-256 against the table at the bottom of these notes.

---

## Verifying the download (recommended)

```powershell
# In PowerShell, in the directory where you saved the installer:
Get-FileHash .\CARE-0.2.0-core-online-Setup.exe -Algorithm SHA256
```

Compare the output with the SHA-256 listed at the bottom of this release. Any mismatch means the file is corrupted or tampered — re-download from this page.

---

## What this release is *not*

- **Not a crash-database scraper.** CARE runs on one workstation and writes redacted artifacts you then move into your own pipeline.
- **Not a privacy-by-magic tool.** The fail-closed gate exists because PII detection is imperfect; CARE refuses to ship a "good enough" redaction. Operators always own the final review.
- **Not a substitute for legal review.** Read the license-and-model-governance doc before enabling any optional model.

---

## Known limitations in this alpha

- Optional ML providers (Piiranha, RoBERTa-NER, Kosmos-2.5, LayoutLM) ship the **loaders** in the `ml` SKU but require manual weight installation.
- Tier-A accuracy numbers are currently from a **synthetic** corpus — a real held-out DOT-partner labelled set is targeted for v0.3.0.
- Per-plugin installers (one core + opt-in `pii-ml`, `vlm`, `presidio`, `ocr-traditional`, `llm-local`, `llm-cloud` bundles) are planned for the next milestone; the current `ml` monolith remains for v0.2.x.
- SignPath enrolment in progress — SmartScreen warning is expected.

## Where to report issues

GitHub Issues: https://github.com/AhmedAredah/care/issues. Please include the contents of **Settings → Diagnostics** (it auto-scrubs paths and PII) when reporting.

---
